$comment: The app's SHAPE vocabulary — the companion to tokens.contract.json.
The token contract stopped the two surfaces' COLOURS from drifting apart. Nothing
stopped their SHAPES, and a design system drew four of them by hand, from reading
this source, with nothing checking the reading. Two of the four were wrong within
a week: it described the inbox row as flat hairline-separated rows (the marketing
site's idiom — `0.5px` appears nowhere in this package) when the real row is a
bordered card in a spaced list, and it generalised the state pattern from the two
warning callouts in LLMSettings into a rule the other 100+ state surfaces do not
follow. Both were plausible. Both were read off this code. Both were false.
So the reading is now written down and checked from both ends:
`evidence` — literal class strings that MUST still be present in `source`.
Checked HERE, in this repo, on the commit that changes the component. If the
app restyles a shape, this fails and the design system has to follow.
`preview` — the CSS the design system's product page MUST use to draw the shape.
Checked THERE, against this file, fetched over https (this repo is public).
If the design system draws something the app does not do, that fails.
Neither half alone is enough: the first catches the app moving, the second catches
the drawing moving. The reason the previous version could be wrong at all is that
it had neither — it had a paragraph promising the shapes were read carefully.
It names no other repository and no private path: it is a list of class names
checked against this package's own components.

chat-user.what: The user's turn is a bubble. It is the only thing in the chat that is.

chat-assistant.what: The assistant's turn has no container at all — plus the AI-disclosure badge, which is not optional.
evidence: class="ai-badge float-left mr-2 mt-[3px] inline-flex items-center rounded-[var(--radius-sm)] border border-accent/40 bg-accent/10 px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-mono uppercase tracking-widest text-accent"

chat-tool.what: A tool call is a left rule, never a card. State lives in the rule's colour AND in a full-strength tick.
evidence: text-[13px] md:text-[11px] text-text-subtle/70 border-l-2 {isError ? 'border-danger/40' : isRunning ? 'border-warning/40' : 'border-border'} pl-3

inbox-row.what: A bordered card in a spaced list — NOT a flat hairline-separated row. Selection is the dominant state.

inbox-bar.what: The toolbar sits on a 40% wash of the subtle ground — enough to separate, not enough to announce itself.

state.what: A tint, a border of the SAME colour at low opacity, and the text in the semantic colour. Not a full-strength border, and never the normal ink.
evidence: bg-danger/10 border border-danger/20 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-danger

checkbox.what: The one real primitive. 16px, and it keeps a visible focus ring.

$comment: Facts the design system states in prose. Prose rots silently; these do not.
Scope is the LIBRARY — src/lib/components + src/lib/primitives, i.e. what pro
actually imports. The standalone app's own routes/ are not part of it (there is
a seventh <style> block in routes/login, and counting it here would make the
design system describe a surface no consumer of this package ever renders).
